creator: freebsd@bitter-almonds.com
text:
in message <201201020630.q026UCXY013307@freefall.freebsd.org>,
wrote f-gnats thusly...
>
> Thank you very much for your problem report.
> It has the internal identification `ports/163765'.
> The individual assigned to look at your
> report is: freebsd-ports-bugs.
>
> You can access the state of your problem report at any time
> via this link:
>
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=163765
>
>       Note: There was a bad value `no=20' for the field `>Confidential:'.
>       It was set to the default value of `yes'.

I don't think mutt had butchered the email as it appears at ...

  http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=163765


I did not this PR to be "Confidential".  Nothing was filled in any
"Fix" or attached.  Sent originally ...

  System: FreeBSD holstein.holy.cow 8.2-STABLE FreeBSD 8.2-STABLE \
  #2: Fri Apr 22 12:33:32 HST 2011 \
  root@holstein.holy.cow:/misc/obj/misc/src/sys/T61-SMP.8 i386


Compile time error message is attached this time.


  - parv
